memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Helsby, Hellsby, Helsbie, Helsbee, Hellsbee and many more. First found in Cheshire where they were anciently seated as Lords of the Manor of Helmsby. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Richard Hellsby who landed in North America in 1710. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). Motto Translated: In God is my hope. Recorded in the spellings of Helsby, Helsbie, and the dialectals Elsby, Elsbie, Elsbury, and possibly others, this is an English surname. It is locational and is probably from the village name of Helsby in the county of Cheshire. It may also be topographical from residence by either a cave near a farm from the Scandanavian 'hellr' and '-bi', a farm, or perhaps from pre-7th century Old English 'aldr-burh' meaning a hill covered with alder trees. Locational surnames names were "from" names. That is to say names that were usually given to people after they moved to another area, the easiest way to identify a stranger being to call him, or sometimes her, by the name of the place from whence they came. Spelling being at best erratic and local dialects very thick, lead to the development of alternative spellings, some far removed from the original. The first recorded spelling of the family name may be that of Agnes Helsby at the church of St Michael Bassishaw in the city of London on 29 May 1580, and later that of Henry Elsby at St James Clerkenwelll, on 20 October 1620 when he married Katherine Pellam. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 - 2014.

zh.Thornton le Moors, Cheshire, England: 英國爾桑頓 勒 波斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alan de Acton of Hellesby於1250年出生在英國爾利斯比時，他的父親Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby54歲，母親Alice de Thornton25歲。他和Beatrix Hatton至少有6個兒子和5個女兒。他去世於1296年在英國爾桑頓 勒 波斯享年46歲。

zh-hans.Thornton le Moors, Cheshire, England: 英国爾桑頓 勒 波斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alan de Acton of Hellesby于1250年出生在英国爾利斯比时，他的父亲Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby54岁，母亲Alice de Thornton25岁。他和Beatrix Hatton至少有6个儿子和5个女儿。他去世于1296年在英国爾桑頓 勒 波斯享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Acton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places, especially in Shropshire and adjacent counties, named Acton. Generally, these are from Old English āc ‘oak’ + tūn ‘settlement’. In Ireland the surname has been established in Connacht since the 17th century.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Alan de Acton of Hellesby was born in 1250, in Helsby, Cheshire, England, his father, Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby, was 54 and his mother, Alice de Thornton, was 25. He had at least 6 sons and 5 daughters with Beatrix Hatton. He died in 1296, in Thornton le Moors, Cheshire, England, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alan de Acton of Hellesby est né en 1250, à Helsby, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Alice de Thornton, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles avec Beatrix Hatton. Il est décédé en 1296, à Thornton le Moors, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alan de Acton of Hellesby 1250 in Helsby, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby 54 und seine Mutter Alice de Thornton 25. Er hatte mit Beatrix Hatton mindestens 6 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb 1296 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Thornton le Moors, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alan de Acton of Hellesby è nato il nel 1250 nel Helsby, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby, aveva 54 anni e il suo madre, Alice de Thornton, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 6 figli e 5 figlie con Beatrix Hatton. Morì nel 1296, nel Thornton le Moors, Cheshire, England, all'età di 46 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Alan de Acton of Hellesby(이)가 1250년 영국 체실르 헬스비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby(은)는 54세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Thornton(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Beatrix Hatton(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1296년 영국 체실르 토른턴 일 무르스에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alan de Acton of Hellesby nasceu em 1250, em Helsby, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Thornton, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas com Beatrix Hatton. Ele faleceu em 1296, em Thornton le Moors, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 46 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alan de Acton of Hellesby родился в 1250 г. в местности хелсби, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Alice de Thornton, 25. У него было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей от Beatrix Hatton. Он умер в 1296 г. в местности таурнтон л мурс, чешир, Англия в возрасте 46 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alan de Acton of Hellesby nació en 1250, en Helsby, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Acton - Lord of Hellesby, tenía 54 años y su mamá, Alice de Thornton, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas con Beatrix Hatton. Murió en 1296, en Thornton le Moors, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 46 años.
